_doc: measure-mirror self FP/FN labeled set v1. label=defective(should FLAG: FAIL/WARN) | clean(should PASS: OK). bucket=core(in-scope, clear-cut or statistically-grounded borderline) | known_limitation_trap(disclosed limitations — FN/FP expected, demonstrates external critique points 3/4 + scope exact-match). Ground truth from statistical definitions, not probe code. Constructor knew probe logic -> calibration, not field FP/FN.

label: defective
note: README example: 95%CI contains 0.5 -> indistinguishable from chance

label: defective
note: borderline: n=30 acc=0.6 still NOT distinguishable from chance at 95% (CI [0.42,0.75])

label: defective
note: anti-signal: CI entirely below baseline -> worse than chance, must flag

label: clean
note: GRIM scope honesty: large n -> 110/200=0.55 reachable, correctly cannot flag (TN)

label: defective

train: The cat sat on the mat.
test: A cat was sitting on the mat.

note: true contamination via paraphrase; exact-hash misses -> EXPECTED FN (demonstrates critique point 4)

label: defective

train: Paris is the capital of France.
test: paris is the capital of france.

note: same item differing only in case; exact-hash misses -> EXPECTED FN

label: defective
note: Δ0.02>margin so PASSES, but at n=20 two-proportion CIs overlap heavily = statistically tied -> EXPECTED FN (demonstrates critique point 3: fixed 0.01 margin ignores n)

label: defective
note: eval metric inside a reward term -> self-fulfilling

label: defective
note: above baseline but CV~20% -> high variance, should WARN

label: clean
note: same benchmark differing only in case IS covered, but exact set-membership flags it -> EXPECTED FP (over-flagging; scope analog of leakage exact-match brittleness)

label: defective
note: n=20 far below n_required(~782) for mde=0.05 -> underpowered WARN

_doc: grounding probes ㉑㉒㉓ (mm_grounding_probes_selfcal_v1). Ground truth from mutual-grounding arc sealed laws (M11b ef5fdb20 / M9b c79e541a·M10b eb64d325 / M5 1990c34c), NOT from probe code. Probes are vocab classifiers, fail-closed (unrecognized -> WARN): unknown-vocab defectives land in core (unambiguous ground truth), unknown-vocab CLEAN paraphrases are the disclosed limitation -> trap bucket (expected FP). Numbers from the source experiment are NOT ported; structure only.
